macro_summary: Rate backdrop (FRED, not live): payrolls +57k (cooling from +129k); unemployment 4.2% (down); last CPI 4.3% YoY (up, stale). Stocks capped a choppy week with modest gains Friday (SPY +0.47%, QQQ +0.36%) as the US-Iran standoff in the Strait of Hormuz showed signs of losing its oil-market punch—WTI fell 1.35% to $71.41 despite fresh missile exchanges. The Fed remained in focus after minutes revealed officials are ‘deeply divided’ on inflation, leaving the July 14 CPI report as the next rate-hike catalyst. China’s temporary helium export ban (per Fox News, July 10) added a new supply-chain risk for chipmakers.
commodities_note: WTI slipped to $71.41, now down ~19% over the month as the Hormuz risk premium continues to erode. Gold softened 0.62% to $4,104, pressured by a 4.57% 10-year yield. The US10Y inched up 0.66% on Friday; money-market rates are topping 5.0%, underscoring the hawkish rate backdrop.

writeup.what_to_do: The stock is in a severe downtrend with no sign of stabilization. No entry until it reclaims the $15 level with volume or shows a capitulation spike. The GaN adoption thesis is too early to justify a bottom call.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 60x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.stale_session_note: No new session since 2026-07-10 — bar date and price identical to the previous report (market closed: holiday/weekend). Technicals are unchanged, already-reported data.
thesis_highlights: PLTR battlefield-AI thesis: (1) Ukraine as live-combat validation, (2) cross-allied interoperability across US Army + NATO as platform moat. Track contract drawdown pace, not deployment announcements.

writeup.headline: PLTR near $126 support, but 60x forward PE and tight invalidation block entry.
writeup.what_to_do: Alex Karp's white paper reframes AI sovereignty, but the 60x multiple and tight invalidation ($126.30, 0.4% below) make R:R unactionable. Sizing R:R to $122.68 is 2.3:1, but the premium to BigTech is extreme. Reassess after Jul 22 earnings for DoD contract drawdown pace.
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 60x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if multiple compresses toward the BigTech cluster median (24.6x) without a corresponding earnings re-rating.

WRD.stale_session_note: No new session since 2026-07-10 — bar date and price identical to the previous report (market closed: holiday/weekend). Technicals are unchanged, already-reported data.
thesis_highlights: WeRide Q1 2026 results (reported May 13 2026): revenue +58% YoY on Robotaxi fleet expansion and L2++ ADAS commercialisation — cite the print specifics when news references it.
WeRide bull case rests on B2B fleet licensing (Uber, Grab, Bosch) + multi-country Robotaxi deployment — evaluate on commercial traction, not autonomy benchmarks vs Waymo/Tesla.
ADR/China delisting risk is a standing consideration — always maintain starter sizing for WRD.

writeup.headline: WRD near $5.18 support, but ADR delisting risk and pre‑profit stage keep it a speculative watch.
writeup.what_to_do: The Geely‑Farizon partnership is positive, but WRD is still pre‑profit and delisting risk looms. Wait for a bounce above $5.96 with volume or news of permit approvals in key markets. No entry before commercial traction is visible.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if WRD fails to reclaim SMA50 ($6.74) on above-average volume within the next 10 sessions.

writeup.headline: SITM -9.5% below 50‑day, 51x forward PE — MEMS timing thesis intact but entry gated on consolidation.
writeup.what_to_do: High forward PE and distorted R:R (6.6:1 headline, 1.02:1 wide stop) block entry. Wait for a better base near $609 or a named hyperscaler design‑win catalyst. The NVDA‑coupling risk makes it a leverage play.
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 51x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

singapore.summary: All three banks are hard‑blocked on extreme extension (RSIs above 80, all >10% above their 50‑day averages). The rally has run ahead of NIM‑compression reality — US10Y at 4.57% remains above the 4.20% threshold for relief, and MAS has not eased S$NEER. No entries until a meaningful pullback.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally cautious — NIM compression from dual channels persists, keeping the cluster in a holding pattern.
singapore.key_development: UOB’s RSI hit 88, the deepest overbought reading in the group, while DBS and OCBC are similarly stretched. No fundamental catalyst has emerged to justify the move.

semiconductors.summary: The cluster is deeply divergent: NVDA reclaimed its 50‑day, while AMD, AVGO, LITE, SNDK and MU are extended or momentum‑blocked. INTC and TSEM drift below their averages. Meta’s in‑house chip news added a fresh competitive wrinkle. The memory supercycle thesis (MU, 000660.KS, SNDK) is intact but entry timing is poor due to extension.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish but tactically dispersed — the memory supercycle and optical interconnects remain the two dominant legs, but custom‑ASIC in‑housing (Meta, Google) adds risk.
semiconductors.key_development: China’s helium export ban and the US‑Iran escalation risk supply‑chain disruption for chip fabrication. ASML and TSMC earnings this week will be the next cluster‑wide catalysts.

big_tech.summary: AMZN, GOOG, and MSFT all consolidated near their 50‑day or below, awaiting earnings. The Azure/OpenAI concentration story softened as GPT‑5.6 integration deepened the partnership, but it still clouds the revenue‑quality picture. PLTR remains extended on valuation. No compelling entries in the group this week.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex commitments are a tailwind for AI infrastructure, but revenue quality and the ROI payoff remain open questions.
big_tech.key_development: Meta’s AI chip production plans (per Reuters) add a new dimension to the in‑housing threat, indirectly pressuring GOOG’s TPU narrative and MSFT’s GPU reliance.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA’s $2B photonics investment partner; rides the same AI optical interconnect wave as LITE.
interconnected.entry_note: Worth scouting if COHR pulls back 8‑10% from current levels post‑S&P 500 inclusion flows, ideally before the next NVDA earnings cycle.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: TSEM shipped 5M coherent PICs to Marvell for AI data center optics — a direct read‑through to the optical networking theme.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if MRVL’s next earnings confirm the data‑center optics ramp above a $250M quarterly run‑rate.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Meta’s 14GW compute push drives demand for high‑bandwidth switches — ANET is the prime networking beneficiary.
interconnected.entry_note: Scout for an entry on a 5‑8% pullback, ideally timed with NVDA Rubin launch updates.

interconnected.name: Super Micro Computer
interconnected.reason: SMCI’s DCBBS blueprint for NVDA Vera Rubin NVL4 positions it for hyperscaler deployments.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit if SMCI breaks above $100 on volume during the Rubin ramp, confirming a new product cycle.

active_risks: US‑Iran military exchanges in the Strait of Hormuz disrupt oil tanker traffic but fail to lift WTI sustainably (now $71.41).
Fed rate‑hike fears dominate as inflation remains above target; June CPI report (Jul 14) is the next catalyst.
China temporarily bans helium exports, threatening semiconductor fabrication gas supply and triggering new supply‑chain risk.
US‑China tech tensions broaden with bipartisan push to ban Chinese EVs and new critical‑mineral export curbs.
geopolitical.market_impact: WTI’s -19% monthly decline signals the market is discounting the Hormuz risk premium. The 10‑year yield at 4.57% keeps SG bank NIMs compressed and semis sensitive to rate‑hike expectations. The helium ban creates a fresh overhang for ASML and other fab‑dependent names.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: China’s helium export ban (per Fox News, July 10) adds a new gas‑supply risk for chip manufacturing. US and Iran exchanged fire overnight, but oil prices fell on Friday.

base.description: Stalemate persists, WTI drifts toward $65 as Iran’s oil leverage wanes; Fed holds but stays hawkish. Semis consolidate ahead of earnings; SG banks stay extended but drift lower.

optimistic.description: Iran deal reached, WTI drops below $60, rate‑cut bets revive. Semis rally broadly, led by NVDA/TSM/ASML, and SG banks finally get NIM relief if the 10‑year falls below 4.20%.

pessimistic.description: Full blockade closes the Strait, WTI spikes above $100, recession fears spike. Semis crash as hyperscaler capex is questioned; SG banks sell off with global risk‑off.

wildcard.description: China helium ban cascades into chip fab shutdowns, creating a global chip shortage. Inflation spikes further, forcing aggressive rate hikes, while semis both benefit (pricing power) and suffer (demand destruction).

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Size small; wait for CPI (Jul 14) and semis earnings to clear. No large entries until the rate picture solidifies.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 7 of 31 names are hard‑blocked on technical extension.
